TimeScalePatch.description: Request to set a controller's simulated-clock speed (simulation-only). The platform relays it to the controller's REST `PUT /sim/time-scale`. `scale` multiplies only the wall-clock tick cadence; it does not change the per-tick simulation step, so determinism is preserved (controller HAL §7). Out-of-range values are rejected with 422. This is a simulation-only diagnostic, not a setpoint — it is an explicit, narrow exception to the platform's setpoint-only downward control (platform constraints §7).

scale.description: Wall-clock cadence multiplier. 1.0 = real-time; <1 slows down (0.5 = half speed); >1 speeds up (2.0, 4.0 = fast-forward). The operator UI offers 0.5/1/2/4/8; direct/headless clients may still request any value in range, including 16/32. Out-of-range is rejected with 422.

TimeScale.description: A controller's simulated-clock speed state, as relayed from the controller (controller-rest `TimeScale`). On GET this is the current scheduler target; on PATCH this is the accepted scheduler target, with the WebSocket status frame / next telemetry snapshot serving as the authoritative observed value. Ephemeral on the controller — resets to 1.0 on restart. Simulated HAL only; a real-hardware controller has no time-scale.

tick_index.description: Monotonic simulation tick counter since controller startup. One tick = one simulated second.

updated_at.description: When the scale was last set, as the controller's clock instant (RFC 3339 UTC, ms).

FleetTimeScaleResult.description: Result of a fleet-wide time-scale change (simulation-only). The platform fans the requested `scale` out to every greenhouse as N independent per-controller writes — there is no shared/master clock — and reports the per-greenhouse outcome. A greenhouse whose controller is offline or on real hardware is reported as not applied, not a request-level failure.

requested_scale.description: The scale requested for the whole fleet.

results.description: Per-greenhouse outcome of the fan-out, one entry per greenhouse attempted.

applied.description: True when the controller accepted the new scale.

scale.description: The controller's resulting scale, or null when not applied (offline, or real hardware → 404).

detail.description: Reason this greenhouse was not applied (e.g. "offline", "not a simulated backend"), or null on success.
